description: Category registry. The six 'seed' categories below are always present. Loop 5 (05-replacement.md, 'Dynamic category discovery') may PROPOSE new categories when a coherent cluster of evidence-backed, adversarially-survived questions does not fit any existing category. Loop 8 persists the live registry to interview_questions_knowledge/categories.json; this file is the seed/default the agent falls back to on a cold start. The dashboard builds its tabs from the live registry, so new categories render automatically with no template edit.

seed_categories.label: SWE System Design
seed_categories.rationale: Top 20 by frequency from ~30 canonical topics.

seed_categories.label: SWE Behavioral
seed_categories.rationale: Top 20 themes scored on framework alignment, not raw frequency.

seed_categories.label: AI/ML Coding
seed_categories.rationale: 20 implementation topics with growing LLM coverage.

seed_categories.label: AI/ML Conceptual
seed_categories.rationale: 20 theory topics with heavy emerging-tech overlap.

dynamic_discovery.description: Rules under which Loop 5 may add a NEW category beyond the seeds. Designed to extend coverage only when evidence demands it, never to churn the taxonomy.

promote_when.note: A 'cluster' is a set of canonical questions sharing a coherent theme (e.g. an OS/concurrency cluster, a SQL/data cluster, a frontend cluster) that does not map to any seed category's subcategory vocabulary.

stability.note: At most one new category per cycle. A discovered category that falls below min_clustered_questions for 2 consecutive cycles is demoted and its questions reassigned to the nearest seed category (or emerging.json).

scoring_modes.standard: Use the Frequency Score formula in 03-scoring.md.
scoring_modes.behavioral: Use the behavioral scoring matrix in 03-scoring.md.
scoring_modes.note: A newly discovered category inherits the 'scoring' mode chosen by Loop 5 at promotion time (default 'standard'). Coding-like clusters should use 'standard'; soft-skill clusters should use 'behavioral'.
